title: Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026: UNHCR & ICVA Open Grants for Local NGOs and CSOs Worldwide
summary: Local and national NGOs working in humanitarian settings now have a major opportunity to secure funding for community protection and awareness initiatives through the Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026. Jointly launched by the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) and the International Council of Voluntary Agencies (ICVA), this initiative is designed to strengthen grassroots efforts aimed at preventing sexual exploitation and abuse (PSEA) in vulnerable communities. The 2026 Call for Proposals is officially open, providing grants for organizations that are developing community-centered outreach campaigns, awareness materials, communication strategies, and systems-strengthening activities tailored to local realities, languages, and cultures. This funding opportunity is particularly important for local humanitarian actors, grassroots organizations, and civil society groups that are directly engaged with displaced populations, refugees, conflict-affected communities, and other vulnerable groups in humanitarian crises. About the Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund The Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund was established in 2020 as a collaborative initiative between ICVA and UNHCR. Since its launch, the Fund has focused on empowering local organizations to lead awareness and prevention efforts against sexual exploitation and abuse within humanitarian settings. The Fund recognizes that local organizations are often best positioned to understand community realities, communicate effectively in local languages, and design culturally appropriate interventions that genuinely resonate with affected populations. The initiative aims to ensure that people receiving humanitarian assistance: Understand their rights Know that humanitarian aid is never conditional on favors or exchanges Have access to safe reporting channels Are aware of available support systems Can participate in community-led protection mechanisms By supporting local leadership, the Fund contributes to a more accountable, transparent, and community-driven humanitarian response system. What Does the Fund Support? The 2026 PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund supports a wide range of locally driven initiatives that improve awareness, communication, and protection systems related to sexual exploitation and abuse prevention. 1. Community-Led Protection Initiatives The Fund prioritizes projects that place communities at the center of protection efforts. Organizations are encouraged to involve affected populations directly in the design and implementation of outreach strategies. Examples include: Community awareness campaigns Peer-to-peer education initiatives Grassroots advocacy programs Survivor-centered communication approaches Community dialogue sessions 2. Locally Tailored Communication Materials Supported projects are expected to create outreach materials adapted to: Local languages Literacy levels Cultural realities Vulnerable population groups Indigenous communication systems Materials may include: Posters Radio programs Comics and illustrations Community theatre Videos Social media campaigns Audio messaging Educational toolkits 3. Strengthening Local Leadership in PSEA The Fund is committed to strengthening local ownership of PSEA initiatives and supporting grassroots organizations in becoming leaders within humanitarian protection systems. This aligns with major international commitments including: The Grand Bargain localization agenda The Global Compact on Refugees Inter-Agency Standing Committee (IASC) localization commitments 4. Open-Source PSEA Materials and Knowledge Sharing All outreach materials created through funded projects become part of a publicly accessible deliverables database. This allows organizations globally to adapt and reuse effective communication tools while reducing duplication and improving consistency in humanitarian messaging. Impact of the PSEA Outreach Fund Since its establishment, the Fund has already demonstrated measurable results across multiple humanitarian contexts worldwide. Key Achievements 65+ PSEA Outreach Projects Funded The initiative has already financed more than 65 grassroots protection and awareness projects implemented by local organizations. Over 2 Million People Reached Fund-supported projects have collectively reached more than 2 million people with awareness messaging and protection information. Outreach Materials Produced in 60+ Languages Projects have successfully developed culturally adapted communication materials in more than 60 languages, significantly improving accessibility and inclusivity. More Than 450 Outreach Materials Developed Organizations have produced hundreds of educational and awareness materials that are now available for adaptation and reuse globally. Independent Evaluation Findings An independent evaluation supported by the UK Foreign, Commonwealth Development Office (FCDO) confirmed the strong impact and strategic importance of the Fund. Major Findings Include: 84% Increase in Community Awareness Organizations reported a dramatic increase in awareness related to sexual exploitation and abuse prevention across communities reached. Increased Reporting Mechanisms Approximately 85% of participating organizations observed moderate or significant increases in reporting of SEA-related incidents after project implementation. Stronger Local Organizations 78% of organizations stated that implementing a Fund-supported project significantly strengthened their internal PSEA capacity. Community Participation Matters 68% of respondents indicated that communities were heavily involved in project design and implementation, increasing trust and effectiveness. Why This Fund Matters in Humanitarian Settings Sexual exploitation and abuse remain major concerns in humanitarian contexts where vulnerable populations often face heightened risks and power imbalances. The PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund addresses these challenges by: Supporting localized protection strategies Increasing awareness among vulnerable groups Promoting safe reporting systems Strengthening accountability Encouraging survivor-centered approaches Building trust between aid providers and communities Importantly, the Fund moves beyond simple awareness campaigns by encouraging projects that address structural issues and power dynamics that contribute to exploitation and abuse. Eligibility and Who Can Apply The 2026 Call for Proposals is primarily aimed at local and national NGOs responding to humanitarian crises. Organizations applying should demonstrate: Community engagement experience Capacity to implement outreach activities Knowledge of local contexts and languages Commitment to protection and safeguarding principles Ability to develop culturally appropriate communication materials Projects should focus on humanitarian settings and prioritize at-risk populations. Application Timeline Applications Opened: 19 May 2026 Application Deadline: 14 June 2026 at 23:59 CET Late applications may not be considered, so organizations are encouraged to begin preparing proposals early. Available Resources for Applicants Applicants can access guidance and application materials in multiple languages, including: English Arabic French The Fund also provides access to: PSEA materials database Project summaries from previous funding rounds ICVA PSEAH publications Application guidance documents Benefits of Participating in the Fund Organizations selected for funding can benefit from: Financial support for PSEA outreach projects Increased organizational visibility Capacity strengthening opportunities Access to global PSEA resources Participation in international humanitarian protection networks Contribution to community-led safeguarding systems Opportunities for long-term partnership development How to Apply Interested organizations should carefully review the official guidance documents and application materials before submitting proposals. Applicants are advised to: Read all application guidance thoroughly Clearly define community needs Demonstrate local engagement Explain sustainability plans Highlight culturally adapted approaches Include measurable outcomes and indicators Apply, Click Here For more information about the proposals, visit the official website Final Thoughts The Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026 represents a significant opportunity for grassroots organizations and humanitarian actors to strengthen protection systems and empower vulnerable communities. By supporting locally led initiatives, UNHCR and ICVA are reinforcing the importance of community ownership, accountability, and culturally grounded communication in preventing sexual exploitation and abuse in humanitarian contexts. Organizations with innovative, community-centered outreach ideas are strongly encouraged to apply before the June 14, 2026 deadline. Opportunity Category Grant Eligible Applicants Local NGOs National NGOs Civil Society Organizations (CSOs) Humanitarian organizations Community-led organizations Grassroots protection groups Eligible Regions Open to organizations operating in humanitarian settings globally. Required Qualifications Experience in humanitarian or protection-related work Capacity to implement community outreach activities Understanding of safeguarding and PSEA principles Ability to develop localized communication materials Strong community engagement approach Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Inter-Agency PSEA Community Outreach and Communication Fund 2026: UNHCR ICVA Open Grants for Local NGOs and CSOs Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Barr Foundation Fellowship 2026: $75,000 Leadership Fellowship for Nonprofit Leaders in Massachusetts
summary: The Barr Foundation has officially reopened applications for the highly anticipated 2026 Barr Fellowship Program, a transformative leadership initiative designed to support nonprofit leaders navigating today’s increasingly demanding social impact landscape. After a strategic review and relaunch, the Barr Fellowship is returning with a renewed vision focused on resilience, renewal, leadership sustainability, collaboration, and relationship-building among nonprofit leaders across Massachusetts. The Fellowship recognizes extraordinary civic and nonprofit leaders while providing substantial financial support, executive coaching, wellness resources, and long-term networking opportunities that strengthen both individuals and organizations. Applications for the 2026 Barr Fellows cohort are now open. About the Barr Fellowship The Barr Fellowship was first launched in 2005 by the Barr Foundation to celebrate and strengthen exceptional leadership across the nonprofit and social impact sectors. Over the years, the Fellowship has supported more than 80 leaders across seven cohorts, creating a vibrant network of changemakers working in different fields and communities throughout Massachusetts. Following the challenges brought about by the COVID-19 pandemic, racial justice movements, increasing climate concerns, and growing pressure on nonprofit organizations, the Foundation conducted an extensive review of the Fellowship program. The result is a refreshed Fellowship experience built specifically to help leaders: Combat burnout and isolation Improve resilience and sustainability Develop collaborative leadership skills Build stronger professional networks Foster healthier organizational cultures Invest in personal well-being and renewal The 2026 Fellowship reflects a growing recognition that nonprofit leaders need more than funding alone — they also need support systems, trusted relationships, wellness opportunities, and sustainable leadership practices. What the Barr Fellowship 2026 Offers Selected Fellows and their organizations will receive a comprehensive package of financial, professional, and leadership support over a 15-month period. 1. $75,000 Organizational Grant Each selected Fellow’s organization will receive a total grant of $75,000, divided into two major components: $25,000 dedicated to the Fellow’s personal well-being, leadership development, and renewal $50,000 allocated to organizational resilience, staff wellness, and operational sustainability This funding structure ensures that both the individual leader and their organization benefit from the Fellowship experience. 2. Executive and Organizational Coaching Participants will receive 50 hours of coaching support, including: Executive coaching for the Fellow Organizational coaching focused on resilience-building and leadership sustainability Strategic guidance to strengthen organizational culture and collaborative leadership practices The coaching component is designed to help leaders navigate complex challenges while developing healthier and more effective leadership approaches. 3. Cohort Learning and Leadership Development Fellows will participate in approximately 16 days of scheduled programming over 15 months. The program includes: Leadership retreats Peer learning opportunities Relationship-building sessions Collaborative leadership development Networking experiences across sectors The cohort model allows Fellows to connect deeply with leaders from diverse backgrounds and disciplines across Massachusetts. 4. Lifelong Alumni Network After completing the program, participants become part of the broader Barr Fellows Network. This alumni network provides ongoing: Collaboration opportunities Peer support Leadership exchange Strategic partnerships Community engagement opportunities The network aims to strengthen long-term collaboration among social impact leaders across the Commonwealth. Program Goals of the Barr Fellowship The Barr Fellowship seeks to empower nonprofit leaders to: Increase Personal and Professional Resilience The program prioritizes leader wellness, helping participants cultivate sustainability, clarity, and renewal in demanding leadership roles. Strengthen Collaborative Leadership Skills Fellows gain tools and experiences that improve teamwork, partnership-building, and cross-sector collaboration. Build Healthy Organizational Cultures The Fellowship encourages organizations to create environments where staff can thrive, contribute meaningfully, and feel supported. Expand Leadership Networks Participants build valuable relationships that foster mutual aid, solidarity, innovation, and long-term social impact collaboration. Why the Fellowship Matters Today Nonprofit organizations continue to face immense pressure due to: Reduced resources Increased community needs Workforce burnout Economic uncertainty Social justice challenges Climate-related concerns DEI backlash and organizational stress The Barr Foundation recognizes that leadership in the social sector can often become isolating and unsustainable without intentional support systems. The Fellowship was redesigned specifically to address these realities by helping leaders prioritize wellness, resilience, connection, and organizational sustainability. Eligibility Criteria for the Barr Fellowship 2026 To qualify for the Fellowship, both the organization and the nominated leader must meet specific eligibility requirements. Organizational Eligibility Requirements Applicant organizations must: Be a nonprofit 501(c)(3) public charity or fiscally sponsored by one Be headquartered in Massachusetts Conduct at least 75% of activities or programming in Massachusetts Have an operating budget between approximately $500,000 and $5 million Demonstrate strong commitment to justice, belonging, and inclusive leadership Maintain adequate staffing and operational capacity during Fellowship participation Organizations That Are Not Eligible The following organizations cannot apply: Government agencies Hospitals K-12 schools Higher education institutions Organizations primarily focused on re-granting funds Individual Eligibility Requirements The organization’s nominated candidate must: Serve as CEO, Executive Director, or Co-Director Have held their current role for at least 3 years Have 3–10 years of nonprofit executive leadership experience Be a full-time resident of New England Work professionally with significant focus on Massachusetts Demonstrate commitment to justice, belonging, and healthy organizational culture Commit fully to all Fellowship activities and participation requirements Barr Fellowship 2026 Application Timeline Applicants should carefully note the following important dates: Key Dates Application Opens: May 14, 2026 Information Session 1: June 11, 2026 (3–4 PM ET) Information Session 2: June 16, 2026 (1–2 PM ET) Application Deadline: June 30, 2026 Advancing Candidates Notification: August 2026 Candidate Calls: August 2026 Finalist Notifications: Early September 2026 Finalist Interviews: Late September to Early October 2026 Cohort and Award Announcement: November 2026 Deadline The application deadline for the Barr Fellowship 2026 is June 30, 2026. Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early and attend one of the scheduled information sessions to better understand the Fellowship expectations and selection process. How to Apply Click here for more and to apply Before applying, candidates should carefully review all eligibility requirements and ensure they can fully participate in the Fellowship’s programming and leadership commitments. The Barr Fellowship represents a unique opportunity for nonprofit leaders to invest in personal renewal, strengthen organizational culture, expand collaborative leadership capacity, and join a powerful network of social impact leaders across Massachusetts. For leaders committed to sustainable change and community impact, this Fellowship offers both transformational support and long-term professional connection opportunities. Click here for more global fellowship opportunities The post Barr Foundation Fellowship 2026: $75,000 Leadership Fellowship for Nonprofit Leaders in Massachusetts appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Democracy Hackathon 2026 in Strasbourg, France | Fully Funded Opportunity to Build Tech Solutions Against Online Hate
summary: The Democracy Hackathon 2026 is now officially open for applications, offering innovators, developers, designers, democracy experts, and human rights advocates an exciting fully funded opportunity to travel to Strasbourg, France, and develop cutting-edge technology solutions to combat online hate speech and strengthen democratic values across Europe. Organized by the Council of Europe under the New Democratic Pact for Europe and jointly with No Hate Speech Week, the hackathon aims to bring together some of the brightest minds in technology, democracy promotion, AI ethics, and human rights advocacy. The prestigious event will take place from 17–19 June 2026 at the Palais de l Europe in Strasbourg, France, under the auspices of the Monaco Presidency of the Committee of Ministers. Selected teams will receive: An all-expenses-paid trip to Strasbourg Accommodation and travel support Expert mentoring Access to leading democracy and technology experts A chance to compete for a €50,000 Microsoft grant Post-event mentorship and implementation support The application deadline is 5 June 2026. About the Democracy Hackathon 2026 The Democracy Hackathon is more than a technology competition. It is a strategic initiative focused on defending democracy in the digital age. As democratic systems worldwide face growing threats from: Online hate speech Disinformation Coordinated harassment Digital violence Polarization Malicious online behaviour The Council of Europe is inviting multidisciplinary teams to create innovative, ethical, and human-rights-centered digital solutions capable of strengthening democratic resilience. The event feeds directly into the Council of Europe’s broader political initiative called the New Democratic Pact for Europe, which seeks to address democratic backsliding and restore public trust in democratic institutions. Why This Hackathon Matters Digital platforms have become central to public debate and democratic participation. However, they have also become spaces where: Hate speech spreads rapidly Coordinated manipulation campaigns flourish Women and minority voices face targeted harassment Privacy risks increase Democratic conversations become increasingly toxic The Democracy Hackathon seeks practical solutions that: Protect freedom of expression Preserve privacy rights Reduce online harm Empower institutions Promote democratic participation Rather than focusing solely on content moderation, the hackathon encourages teams to rethink how technology can positively transform online communities and democratic engagement. Key Benefits of Participating Selected teams will receive exceptional opportunities and support. 1. Fully Funded Participation The Council of Europe will provide: Full travel coverage Accommodation support Access to the live event in Strasbourg This removes financial barriers and ensures broad participation. 2. Compete for a €50,000 Microsoft Grant Winning teams will share a: €50,000 Microsoft grant The grant is intended to support the further development and implementation of winning ideas and prototypes. 3. Mentorship From Leading Experts Participants will receive guidance from experts affiliated with: Technical University of Munich University of Strasbourg Democracy Reporting International Institute for Strategic Dialogue These mentors will support teams both before and after the hackathon. 4. Networking Opportunities Participants will engage with: Researchers Civic tech experts Human rights advocates Policymakers AI specialists Democracy practitioners This creates valuable opportunities for future collaborations and career growth. 5. Real-World Impact The hackathon’s outcomes will directly contribute to: Democratic policy consultations Online safety strategies Human-rights-based digital governance Participants will help shape future democratic tools and frameworks in Europe. Theme: “Hack the Hate, Renew Democracy” The central theme of the hackathon focuses on building democratic resilience through ethical technology. Participants are expected to create solutions that: Detect harmful online behaviour Protect vulnerable communities Improve institutional responses Encourage healthier digital engagement Balance innovation with privacy and human rights Core Thematic Goals Projects should focus on one or more of the following objectives. 1. Data Privacy Protection Teams are encouraged to: Develop tools that identify hate speech Preserve user privacy Prevent harmful re-identification Balance utility with data protection 2. Combating Hate Speech Participants may create systems that: Detect coordinated harassment Identify harmful narratives Track cross-platform abuse patterns 3. Preventing Malicious Doxing Solutions should ensure: Anonymous users remain protected Detection tools are not weaponized against vulnerable individuals 4. Policy and Institutional Support Projects can help: Electoral commissions Policy actors Public institutions Trusted flaggers Better detect, classify, and respond to harmful online behaviour. 5. Social Transformation The hackathon strongly encourages: Intersectional approaches Inclusive design Community empowerment Educational interventions Positive digital engagement models Democracy Hackathon Challenges Participants must choose one of four official challenge tracks. 1. Privacy-Preserving Hate Speech Detection Challenge (PrivHSD) Mentored by researchers from the Technical University of Munich. Objective: Build hate speech detection systems that: Maintain strong privacy protections Operate independently of user identity Avoid harmful surveillance outcomes Expected Outputs: Prototype Demonstrable concept Research note Privacy-preserving architecture 2. Coordinated Inauthentic Behaviour Identification Mentored by the Institute for Strategic Dialogue. Objective: Create tools capable of: Detecting coordinated online campaigns Tracking hate narratives across platforms Identifying gendered harassment campaigns Potential Deliverables: Monitoring dashboard Narrative clustering systems Visualized analytical tools 3. Reporting and Response Systems for Online Hate Mentored by Democracy Reporting International. Objective: Develop systems that: Transform online reports into structured evidence Support electoral commissions Improve referral and escalation pathways Expected Outputs: MVPs Wireframes Referral systems Classification frameworks 4. Think Out of the Box Challenge Mentored by the University of Strasbourg. Objective: Create transformative approaches that: Prevent hate speech Contextualize harmful content Promote empathy and dialogue Empower communities Potential Solutions: Educational tools Social impact platforms Policy frameworks Moderation interfaces Creative interventions Who Should Apply? The hackathon is designed for multidisciplinary teams of 3–4 members. Ideal teams should include: 1. Technologist / Backend Developer Responsible for: AI systems NLP tools Detection algorithms Technical implementation 2. Democracy or Policy Expert Responsible for: Human rights alignment Democratic frameworks Governance insights Ethical guidance 3. Designer / UX/UI Innovator Responsible for: User experience Interface design Accessibility Visualization Teams may also add a fourth member if needed. Gender balance within teams is strongly encouraged. How to Apply Step 1: Form a Team Applicants must create a team of: 3–4 members Diverse skill backgrounds Step 2: Submit a Concept Summary Teams must prepare a concept summary of no more than two pages. The proposal should include: Problem statement Proposed solution Human rights considerations Technical overview Expected impact User experience strategy Step 3: Selection and Warm-Up Session Selected teams will: Join a virtual preparation session Refine their ideas with mentors Prepare for the live Strasbourg event Apply: Click Here to learn more and apply. Important Dates Application Deadline: 5 June 2026 Event Dates: 17–19 June 2026 Location: Palais de l’Europe, Strasbourg, France What Makes This Opportunity Unique? Unlike traditional hackathons focused only on coding, the Democracy Hackathon combines: Technology Human rights Democracy Ethics Public policy Social innovation Participants are not just building apps — they are designing tools that could influence the future of democratic governance and digital safety across Europe. This hackathon offers a rare opportunity to: Collaborate internationally Engage with policymakers Receive expert mentorship Build impactful civic technology Gain visibility in the democracy and human-rights space For young innovators passionate about democracy, AI ethics, digital rights, and social impact, this is one of the most meaningful technology competitions of 2026. Discover more competition opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post Democracy Hackathon 2026 in Strasbourg, France | Fully Funded Opportunity to Build Tech Solutions Against Online Hate appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ETH Zurich PhD Scholarship 2026 in Agricultural Economics and Climate Policy in Switzerland | Fully Funded 4-Year Doctoral Opportunity
summary: ETH Zurich has officially announced a fully funded PhD Scholarship opportunity for students interested in Agricultural Economics, Climate Policy, Sustainability, and Climate Change Mitigation in Swiss agriculture. The doctoral position is hosted within the Agricultural Economics and Policy Group led by Professor Robert Finger and forms part of the major national research initiative known as the ACHIEVE Project: “Pathways to a Net-Zero Target for Hard-to-Abate Swiss Emissions.” This prestigious four-year doctoral opportunity offers researchers the chance to work on one of the most urgent global challenges: reducing greenhouse gas emissions in agri-food systems while developing practical and evidence-based climate policy solutions. The position is based in Zurich, Switzerland, and provides: Full funding for four years Competitive ETH Zurich salary Research and training opportunities International collaboration Strong academic mentorship Career development support The application deadline is 26 June 2026. About ETH Zurich ETH Zurich is internationally recognized as one of the world’s leading universities in science, technology, sustainability, and innovation. The university is known for: Cutting-edge research Academic excellence Global scientific impact Innovation-driven education Interdisciplinary collaboration ETH Zurich hosts over 30,000 students and researchers from more than 120 countries and consistently ranks among the top universities globally. The institution is committed to: Sustainability Climate neutrality Equal opportunity Diversity and inclusion Independent and innovative thinking Its research environment encourages collaboration across academia, industry, government, and civil society to solve major global challenges. About the ACHIEVE Project The PhD scholarship is funded under the national research initiative: “Pathways to a Net-Zero Target for Hard-to-Abate Swiss Emissions” (ACHIEVE) The ACHIEVE project brings together: 29 member entities 27 collaboration partners Researchers Public sector institutions Industry actors Civil society stakeholders Policymakers The initiative focuses on developing: Net-zero emission strategies for Switzerland Climate mitigation pathways Evidence-based policy recommendations Sustainable transition frameworks Within this project, the Agricultural Economics and Policy Group at ETH Zurich leads a work package focused on reducing greenhouse gas emissions within Swiss agri-food systems. Research Focus of the PhD Position The doctoral student will conduct research on: Climate change mitigation in agriculture Agricultural economics and policy Behavioural responses to climate policy Sustainable agri-food systems Farmer decision-making processes The project specifically investigates the: Economic aspects of climate mitigation Policy effectiveness and efficiency Behavioural dimensions of environmental action Impact of climate policies on Swiss agriculture Key Research Areas The selected PhD candidate will work on topics such as: Greenhouse gas emission reduction strategies Farmer behaviour and climate adaptation Agricultural policy instruments Climate-related decision-making Sustainability transitions in agriculture Data-driven agricultural policy analysis Economic incentives for environmental action Research Methodology and Activities The doctoral project combines: Economic theory Data collection Survey research Behavioural experiments Quantitative analysis Stakeholder engagement Major Research Activities Include: 1. Conducting Surveys and Choice Experiments The candidate will: Design and implement farmer surveys Conduct behavioural choice experiments Gather primary data from Swiss farmers 2. Data Analysis Responsibilities include: Analyzing survey data Working with secondary datasets Conducting economic and statistical analysis 3. Stakeholder Interaction The researcher will engage with: Farmers Policymakers Agricultural stakeholders Sustainability experts 4. Collaboration with Researchers The candidate will collaborate with: PhD students Postdoctoral researchers International project teams Interdisciplinary research groups 5. Student Supervision The doctoral student will also: Supervise bachelor students Support master’s students PhD Training and Academic Development As part of the doctoral trajectory, the selected candidate will: Complete 18 ECTS of PhD-level coursework Participate in advanced academic training Engage in interdisciplinary learning opportunities ETH Zurich provides extensive mentoring and professional development support throughout the program. Duration and Start Date Duration: Fully funded for four years Employment Type: Full-time Fixed-term position Planned Start Period: Last quarter of 2026 (October–December) Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the following criteria. Educational Qualification Candidates should hold: A Master’s degree in Agricultural Economics, or A closely related discipline Applicants close to completing their Master’s degree may also apply. Language Requirements Because the project involves direct engagement with Swiss farmers and stakeholders, applicants must demonstrate: C1 proficiency in German or French Strong English communication and academic writing skills are also highly beneficial. Skills and Competencies Applicants should possess: Strong analytical skills Quantitative research abilities Academic writing competence Interest in climate policy and sustainability Ability to work in interdisciplinary environments Benefits of the ETH Zurich PhD Scholarship ETH Zurich offers one of the most attractive doctoral research environments in Europe. Key Benefits Include: 1. Fully Funded Position Competitive salary according to ETH Zurich standards Full-time funding for four years 2. Research Excellence Access to world-class research infrastructure Collaboration with leading researchers 3. Personalized Mentorship Strong academic supervision Career guidance and mentoring 4. International and Diverse Environment Multicultural research community Interdisciplinary collaboration opportunities 5. Career Development Support The program prepares graduates for careers in: Academia Government Industry International organizations Sustainability policy institutions 6. Continuous Training Opportunities PhD-level courses Skills development workshops Academic networking opportunities ETH Zurich’s Commitment to Diversity and Sustainability ETH Zurich strongly promotes: Equality of opportunity Inclusive academic culture Respect for diversity Sustainability-focused research The university encourages applicants from diverse backgrounds and fosters a research environment where all staff and students can thrive. Required Application Documents Applicants must submit the following as a single PDF: Cover letter Curriculum Vitae (CV) Contact details of two referees Academic transcripts Application Deadline Deadline: 26 June 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply early and carefully prepare strong research-oriented application materials. How to Apply Interested candidates should: Visit the ETH Zurich online application portal Prepare all required documents Submit the application online before the deadline Important: Applications submitted by email or post will not be accepted Apply HERE Why This Opportunity Is Important Climate change mitigation in agriculture is one of the most pressing global policy challenges today. Agriculture plays a critical role in greenhouse gas emissions, biodiversity protection, food security, and sustainability transitions. This ETH Zurich PhD Scholarship provides researchers with the opportunity to: Contribute to impactful climate research Influence future agricultural policies Work directly with stakeholders Develop practical sustainability solutions Build a globally respected academic career For students passionate about sustainability, agricultural economics, climate governance, and environmental policy, this doctoral opportunity represents an exceptional pathway into high-impact international research. For more information, visit the official website Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE The post ETH Zurich PhD Scholarship 2026 in Agricultural Economics and Climate Policy in Switzerland | Fully Funded 4-Year Doctoral Opportunity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: EU Delegation to China Funded Internship 2026 in Beijing: Fully Funded Opportunity for Young Graduates Interested in Diplomacy, International Relations, Communications, and Translation.
summary: The European Union Delegation to China has announced an exciting funded internship opportunity for young graduates who are passionate about international relations, diplomacy, communications, media, translation, and EU-China cooperation. The internship program will take place in Beijing, China, and is expected to begin in October 2026. This prestigious internship offers selected candidates the chance to work directly within the EU Delegation to China, gaining practical experience inside an international diplomatic mission while contributing to impactful projects and activities. Interns will have the opportunity to engage with political affairs, media and public diplomacy, translation work, and intercultural communication in a highly professional international environment. The internship will last for up to six months, and selected candidates will receive a monthly grant to help cover living expenses during their stay in Beijing. For graduates looking to build careers in diplomacy, public policy, international communications, or global affairs, this internship provides invaluable exposure and professional development opportunities. About the EU Delegation to China Internship 2026 The EU Delegation to China represents the European Union in China and works to strengthen diplomatic, political, economic, and cultural relations between the European Union and China. Through this internship program, the Delegation seeks motivated young graduates who can support its ongoing activities while gaining first-hand experience in international diplomacy. Interns will work closely with professionals and experts across various departments within the delegation. The experience is designed to provide practical training, mentorship, and exposure to international policy-making processes and diplomatic engagement. The internship program is especially suitable for graduates with academic backgrounds or professional interests in: International Relations Diplomacy Political Science European Studies Communications Journalism Media and Public Relations Translation and Interpretation Chinese Studies Language and Linguistics Internship Areas and Responsibilities Selected interns may contribute to one or more of the following areas: 1. Political Affairs and EU-China Relations Interns supporting political affairs will assist in monitoring political developments, conducting research, preparing reports, and contributing to diplomatic initiatives related to EU-China relations. Responsibilities may include: Researching political and international affairs topics Drafting reports and policy briefs Supporting meetings and diplomatic events Monitoring developments related to EU-China cooperation Assisting with communication between departments and stakeholders 2. Press, Public Information, and Social Media Candidates interested in communications and media will support the Delegation’s public diplomacy and digital communication activities. Tasks may include: Managing and creating social media content Supporting public information campaigns Drafting press materials and communication content Monitoring media coverage Assisting with event communication and outreach 3. Translation, Interpretation, and Language Support Interns with strong language skills may assist with translation and interpretation work to support communication between English-speaking and Chinese-speaking stakeholders. Responsibilities may include: Translating official documents and communication materials Assisting during meetings and events Providing interpretation support Reviewing language accuracy and consistency Supporting multilingual communication initiatives Financial Benefits One of the major highlights of this internship is that it is funded. Selected candidates will receive a monthly grant intended to help with living expenses during the internship period in Beijing. Benefits include: Monthly financial support Hands-on experience in a diplomatic mission International work exposure Professional networking opportunities Practical experience in diplomacy and communications Opportunity to strengthen international career prospects Exposure to EU-China diplomatic relations Internship Location EU Delegation to China Beijing, China Beijing provides interns with the opportunity to experience one of the world’s most influential political and cultural capitals while working in an international environment. Eligibility Requirements Applicants interested in the internship should meet the following requirements: Hold a relevant university degree Have strong English drafting and writing skills Be fluent in Chinese Possess excellent communication skills Demonstrate interest in diplomacy, international affairs, communications, or translation Hold a valid visa and legal authorization to stay in China throughout the internship period Candidates who demonstrate professionalism, adaptability, intercultural communication abilities, and strong analytical skills may have an added advantage during the selection process. Why You Should Apply This internship is a valuable opportunity for young graduates seeking international professional experience. Working within the EU Delegation to China allows interns to develop practical skills, build international networks, and gain direct insight into diplomatic operations. Some reasons why this internship stands out include: Exposure to International Diplomacy Interns gain practical understanding of how diplomatic missions operate and engage in international cooperation. Career Development Opportunities The internship strengthens professional profiles for careers in diplomacy, communications, international organizations, media, or public policy. Multicultural Experience Participants work in a diverse and international environment while experiencing life in Beijing. Professional Networking Interns interact with diplomats, policy experts, communication professionals, and international stakeholders. Skill Development The experience enhances research, communication, translation, analytical, and public diplomacy skills. Application Deadline The application deadline for the EU Delegation to China Funded Internship 2026 is: 12 June 2026 at 17:00 Beijing Time Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid last-minute delays. How to Apply Interested candidates should apply by email using the contact below: Email: delegation-china-hr@eeas.europa.eu Official application and information link: EU Delegation to China Internship Information Applicants should ensure they prepare all required documents and verify their visa and legal stay eligibility before applying. Final Thoughts The EU Delegation to China Funded Internship 2026 presents an exceptional opportunity for ambitious graduates who aspire to build careers in international relations, diplomacy, communications, or translation. Beyond financial support, the internship offers meaningful professional exposure and the chance to contribute to real diplomatic and international cooperation efforts. Graduates with strong English and Chinese language skills, excellent communication abilities, and a passion for international engagement are strongly encouraged to apply before the deadline. FOR MORE INFORMATION ABOUT THIS.
