title: University of Trieste Opens Applications for 15 Fully Research-Focused PhD Programmes in Italy for 2026/2027
summary: The University of Trieste has officially launched its highly anticipated Call for Applications for the 42nd Cycle of PhD Programmes for the 2026/2027 academic year. This prestigious doctoral recruitment initiative presents a major opportunity for graduates, researchers, and aspiring academics from around the world to pursue advanced research training in one of Europe’s most research-intensive environments. Located in northeastern Italy, the University of Trieste is internationally recognized for academic excellence, interdisciplinary collaboration, and strong partnerships with scientific institutions across Europe. The latest call includes 15 doctoral programmes spanning science, engineering, medicine, law, humanities, artificial intelligence, environmental studies, and social sciences. For students and professionals seeking funded PhD opportunities in Europe, this call stands out as one of the most comprehensive and competitive doctoral opportunities available in Italy this year. Deadline for Applications The official application deadline is: 25 May 2026 at 12:59 PM (local time) Applicants are strongly encouraged to complete and submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute technical difficulties. Why Study at the University of Trieste? The University of Trieste continues to attract international researchers because of its strong research culture, global outlook, and strategic location within one of Europe’s leading scientific ecosystems. Trieste is widely regarded as one of the continent’s most research-oriented cities and hosts numerous internationally respected institutions and innovation hubs, including AREA Science Park and several advanced research centers. Pursuing a PhD at the University of Trieste offers students the opportunity to engage in cutting-edge research while benefiting from Italy’s rich academic heritage and high quality of life. Key Benefits of the PhD Programmes Applicants selected into the doctoral programmes may benefit from: Access to internationally competitive research environments Opportunities for fully funded doctoral scholarships Collaboration with leading researchers and institutions Strong interdisciplinary learning and research exposure Potential industry-linked research opportunities Online interview options for international applicants Access to advanced laboratories and research infrastructure European academic networking opportunities A multicultural and globally connected academic community Living and studying in one of Italy’s intellectually vibrant cities List of Available PhD Programmes The 42nd Cycle of PhD Programmes includes the following doctoral fields: Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics Applied Data Science and AI Physics Nanotechnology Chemistry Circular Economy Civil-Environmental Engineering and Architecture Industrial and Information Engineering Earth Science, Fluid-Dynamics and Mathematics: Interactions and Methods Health and Biomedical Sciences Molecular Biomedicine Personalized Medicine and Innovative Therapies Neural and Cognitive Sciences Biodiversity, Evolution and Adaptation Humanities, Law, and Social Sciences Philosophy, History and Political Studies Advanced Interdisciplinary Legal Studies Transcultural and Transmedia Studies: Literature, Linguistics, Interpreting and Translation This wide range of disciplines makes the opportunity suitable for candidates from nearly every academic background. Who Should Apply? The University of Trieste PhD programmes are ideal for: Graduates interested in pursuing academic research careers Individuals passionate about innovation and scientific discovery Researchers seeking interdisciplinary collaboration Students aiming to study in Europe Professionals looking to deepen expertise in specialized fields Candidates interested in Artificial Intelligence, medicine, law, engineering, humanities, and environmental science The call is open to both Italian and international applicants. Eligibility Requirements To qualify for admission into the doctoral programmes, applicants must hold an academic qualification that grants access to doctoral studies. Eligible qualifications include: An Italian Laurea Magistrale degree An equivalent foreign university degree Any academic qualification recognized for doctoral study access in the country where it was awarded Applicants should carefully review the requirements for their selected programme, as some doctoral tracks may have additional eligibility conditions. Selection Process Admission into the University of Trieste PhD programmes is highly competitive and based on academic merit. The selection process may include: 1. Academic Evaluation Applicants’ educational background, transcripts, and qualifications will be reviewed. 2. Research Proposal Assessment Certain programmes may require candidates to submit a detailed research proposal outlining intended research interests and objectives. 3. Interview Shortlisted applicants may be invited for interviews conducted either: In person, or Via videoconference for international candidates Each doctoral programme has specific admission procedures and evaluation criteria outlined in the official programme annexes. Funding Opportunities Available One of the major highlights of this doctoral call is the availability of funded positions. Many selected candidates may receive: Fully funded PhD scholarships Tuition fee waivers Social security coverage Research funding support Opportunities linked to specialized research projects Potential collaborations with industry and innovation sectors The number and structure of funded positions vary across programmes. Required Application Documents Depending on the chosen programme, applicants may need to submit: Academic transcripts Degree certificates Curriculum Vitae (CV) Motivation letter Research proposal Reference letters Language proficiency documents Applicants are advised to carefully consult programme-specific requirements before applying. Why This Opportunity Matters The University of Trieste’s 2026/2027 doctoral call represents more than a standard academic opportunity. It offers a pathway into Europe’s growing research ecosystem, providing students with access to international collaborations, high-impact scientific work, and professional advancement opportunities. For candidates seeking a fully immersive research experience in Italy, the University of Trieste provides a strong combination of academic excellence, interdisciplinary innovation, and global visibility. The broad range of disciplines also ensures opportunities for students from both STEM and non-STEM backgrounds. How to Apply Applications must be submitted online through the official University of Trieste admissions portal. Official Application Portal University of Trieste Admissions Portal Official Deadline 25 May 2026 at 12:59 PM (local time) For more information about this opportunity click here Discover more PhD Scholarship opportunities on OFY here The post University of Trieste Opens Applications for 15 Fully Research-Focused PhD Programmes in Italy for 2026/2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Call for Applications: SEAP Fellows 2026 — Fully Remote Human Rights Fellowship for Early-Career Professionals in Asia-Pacific
summary: The 2026 SEAP Fellowship Programme by Nationality for All (NFA) presents an outstanding opportunity for early-career human rights professionals across the Asia-Pacific region to contribute to one of the region’s most significant research initiatives on statelessness, nationality rights, and legal identity systems. As part of its continued work on the Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP), NFA is recruiting two full-time fellows for a three-month remote fellowship running from June 15, 2026 to September 11, 2026. The programme is designed to strengthen regional research, advocacy, and knowledge production concerning statelessness and exclusion across Southeast Asia, East Asia, and Central Asia. Applications close on May 25, 2026 (EOD Bangkok Time). What is the SEAP Fellowship Programme? The SEAP Fellowship Programme is a structured research fellowship aimed at supporting the development and verification of the Second Edition of the Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP). The programme allows selected fellows to work directly with an international human rights organization while gaining hands-on experience in: Human rights research Factchecking methodologies Citation management Legal and policy analysis Website content updating Research documentation Regional advocacy initiatives The fellowship is entirely remote, making it accessible to qualified candidates throughout the Asia-Pacific region. About the Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP) The Statelessness Encyclopedia Asia Pacific (SEAP) is one of the region’s most comprehensive research resources documenting issues related to: Statelessness Nationality laws Citizenship rights Legal identity systems Human rights exclusion Discrimination affecting vulnerable populations SEAP serves as a major regional knowledge platform that supports researchers, policymakers, advocates, civil society actors, and human rights organizations working to address nationality and identity-related challenges across Asia-Pacific. Over the years, the project has compiled extensive information on populations affected by statelessness and nationality discrimination throughout the region. The Second Edition of SEAP aims to further strengthen this research through updated factchecking, legal verification, and citation integration. Fellowship Duration and Structure The fellowship will run for three months on a full-time basis. Fellowship Timeline Start Date: June 15, 2026 End Date: September 11, 2026 Duration: 3 Months Mode: Remote (Work-from-home) Although the programme is remote, fellows may occasionally participate in in-person meetings if necessary. Monthly Stipend Selected fellows will receive a monthly stipend ranging between: AUD 200 – AUD 400 per month The exact amount will depend on the fellow’s location. Key Responsibilities of SEAP Fellows The selected fellows will support the NFA research team in updating and verifying SEAP’s Second Edition. 1. Factchecking and Additional Research Fellows will: Review existing SEAP country factsheets Conduct supplementary desk research Verify legal, policy, and statistical information Identify outdated or inconsistent information Cross-check regional human rights documentation 2. Website and Report Updates Responsibilities include: Updating SEAP website content Revising research reports Ensuring consistency across materials Coordinating edits with the NFA team 3. Citation Integration and Verification Fellows will also: Add missing citations Verify citation accuracy Ensure source integrity Maintain consistency in referencing standards Detailed Fellowship Work Plan Month 1: Orientation and Factchecking During the first month, fellows will receive onboarding and practical training on: Factchecking methodologies Citation systems Website updating procedures Research verification techniques Fellows will begin reviewing and factchecking assigned regional content during this stage. Month 2: Website and Report Revisions The second month focuses on: Updating website materials Revising reports based on verified findings Collaborating with the NFA research team Improving content consistency and accuracy Month 3: Citation Integration and Final Verification The final month involves: Integrating citations into SEAP content Conducting final quality checks Verifying accuracy across reports and website entries Finalizing updates for publication Training and Mentorship Selected fellows will receive extensive support throughout the programme, including: A detailed factchecking template and research guide Training on citation standards Guidance on website updating systems Ongoing mentorship from the NFA research team Continuous feedback and support This makes the programme highly beneficial for individuals seeking practical experience in international human rights research and documentation. Eligibility Criteria for SEAP Fellows 2026 Applicants are expected to demonstrate strong academic, analytical, and organizational abilities. Required Skills Candidates should possess: Strong desk research skills Excellent analytical ability Good writing skills Attention to detail Strong organizational skills Effective time-management abilities Ability to synthesize information clearly Since the fellowship is fully remote, applicants must also be: Self-driven Proactive Comfortable working independently Reliable in meeting deadlines Consistent in remote communication Academic and Knowledge Background Applicants should ideally have academic or professional exposure to: Human rights Law Public policy International relations Related disciplines Knowledge of the following areas is highly desirable: Citizenship rights Statelessness Legal identity systems However, prior expertise in these topics is not mandatory. Preferred Experience Although prior professional experience is not required, applicants with experience in the following areas may have an advantage: Human rights initiatives Research projects Civil society organizations Non-profit organizations Advocacy programmes Importantly, persons with lived experience of statelessness will receive priority consideration. Why You Should Apply for the SEAP Fellowship Programme The SEAP Fellowship offers more than just research experience. It provides fellows with an opportunity to directly contribute to meaningful regional advocacy work while building highly transferable professional skills. Benefits of the Fellowship Participants will have the opportunity to: Contribute to a major regional human rights initiative Gain expertise in nationality rights and statelessness Develop professional research and factchecking skills Learn citation management and documentation systems Work with an international human rights organization Build experience in regional advocacy and knowledge production Strengthen their professional profile in human rights research For early-career professionals interested in international development, policy research, migration studies, citizenship rights, or legal identity systems, this fellowship provides valuable practical exposure. Required Application Documents Applicants must submit the following materials: Updated CV Cover Letter One original writing sample Application Deadline The deadline for submitting applications is: May 25, 2026 (End of Day Bangkok Time) Late submissions may not be considered. How to Apply Interested applicants should send all required application documents via email to: info@nationalityforall.org Application – SEAP Fellows 2026 For more information about the organization and fellowship programme, visit Nationality for All (NFA) Official Website . The SEAP Fellowship Programme 2026 represents a valuable opportunity for emerging human rights professionals across Asia-Pacific to gain meaningful research experience while contributing to an important regional initiative focused on statelessness, nationality rights, and legal identity systems. The fully remote structure, practical training, mentorship opportunities, and direct involvement in a major research platform make this fellowship especially attractive for individuals seeking to build careers in human rights, policy research, advocacy, and international development. For more information about this click here . Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY here The post Call for Applications: SEAP Fellows 2026 — Fully Remote Human Rights Fellowship for Early-Career Professionals in Asia-Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: JUST Fellowship 2026 in India: Fully Funded Field-Based Fellowship on Just and Sustainable Transitions
summary: The Centre for Financial Accountability (CFA), in collaboration with Samata, has officially launched applications for the Just and Sustainable Transitions (JUST) Fellowship 2026. This unique four-month fellowship programme is designed to support field-based research, storytelling, and documentation around ecological, economic, and social transitions taking place across India. The JUST Fellowship 2026 offers selected fellows the opportunity to engage directly with communities experiencing the impacts of mining, industrialization, climate transitions, ecological degradation, and shifting livelihoods. Through grounded research and community engagement, fellows will contribute to shaping conversations around equitable, democratic, and ecologically sustainable futures. The fellowship particularly focuses on understanding how communities across India are navigating the realities of energy transitions, resource extraction, environmental degradation, labour transitions, governance challenges, and alternative development pathways. About the JUST Fellowship 2026 The Just and Sustainable Transitions (JUST) Fellowship is a field-based initiative rooted in the idea that “just transition” goes far beyond simply replacing fossil fuels with renewable energy. The fellowship explores broader structural and societal questions including: Livelihoods and labour transitions Land rights and governance Ecological sustainability Community participation Public finance and welfare systems Resource governance Environmental justice Decentralised economies Community-led alternatives Across India, communities are facing rapid transformations driven by: Mining projects Industrial expansion Infrastructure development Agricultural transitions Conservation projects Energy transition corridors While many of these changes bring economic shifts, they also often result in: Displacement Ecological destruction Loss of livelihoods Increasing inequalities Social and environmental conflicts At the same time, many grassroots organisations, local communities, and collectives are creating innovative alternatives rooted in ecological restoration, democratic governance, and sustainable livelihoods. The JUST Fellowship aims to document these realities through immersive field-based work and accessible storytelling. Fellowship Focus Areas The fellowship encourages applications exploring a wide range of transition-related themes, including but not limited to: 1. Mining and Resource Extraction Regions Research may focus on communities affected by mining operations, extraction industries, and resource conflicts. 2. Energy Transition Corridors Applicants may explore how renewable energy projects, energy infrastructure, and decarbonization efforts are impacting local populations. 3. Industrial and Emerging Industrial Zones Projects may investigate industrial expansion, labour transitions, environmental impacts, and governance structures. 4. Agriculture and Agroecological Transitions The fellowship supports work related to sustainable agriculture, agroecology, food systems, and rural livelihoods. 5. Ecological Restoration and Conservation Landscapes Fellows may document community-led conservation efforts, restoration projects, and biodiversity initiatives. 6. Transport, Infrastructure, and Urban Transitions Research can examine the social and environmental consequences of urbanisation and infrastructure expansion. 7. Post-Extractive Futures and Community Governance Applicants are encouraged to explore alternative models of development and community-led governance systems. 8. Public Finance and Welfare Systems Projects may analyse financial mechanisms, public spending, institutional systems, and welfare approaches connected to transition processes. Fellowship Duration and Benefits Selected fellows will receive: Fellowship Duration Four-month fellowship programme Fellowship period: July to October 2026 Financial Support Monthly stipend of Rs. 40,000 Travel support of up to Rs. 10,000 (where required) Mentorship Support Participants may receive mentorship support based on: Project needs Availability of thematic mentors Area of specialization The mentorship component is intended to strengthen fellows’ research, documentation, and storytelling capacities. Who Can Apply? The JUST Fellowship 2026 is open to Indian citizens with experience or demonstrated interest in: Livelihoods Labour rights Environment Ecology Governance Public policy Media Community work Social justice Development studies The organisers particularly encourage applications from: Researchers Journalists Independent writers Practitioners Grassroots workers Community organisers Applicants should ideally have: 2–3 years of relevant experience Interest in field-based research Strong documentation and writing abilities Commitment to ecological and social justice issues Fellowship Outputs Selected fellows will be expected to produce: 1. Long-Form Research Article One in-depth article between 6,000–8,000 words 2. Public-Facing Article or Op-Ed One concise article between 800–1,200 words Adapted for public engagement and wider dissemination The long-form outputs from all fellows will be compiled into a collective publication by the organisers. Why the JUST Fellowship Matters The JUST Fellowship stands out because it bridges multiple fields rather than fitting solely into journalism, academia, or policy research. The programme creates a space where: Field realities meet public discourse Research connects with storytelling Community experiences shape policy conversations Ecological justice becomes central to transition debates It also contributes to growing conversations around: Climate justice Democratic governance Sustainable development Equitable transitions Community-led resilience Public accountability Application Deadline Applications for the JUST Fellowship 2026 close on: 14 June 2026 Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare their applications early. How to Apply Applicants can access the fellowship concept notes in: English Hindi Apply Here For more information about this fellowship visit here Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY on here The post JUST Fellowship 2026 in India: Fully Funded Field-Based Fellowship on Just and Sustainable Transitions appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026: Global Innovation Challenge in Switzerland for Future Entrepreneurs and Problem Solvers
summary: The ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026 is now open for ambitious innovators, entrepreneurs, students, researchers, and creative thinkers from around the world who are ready to tackle real-world problems in one of Europe’s most immersive innovation experiences. Hosted by the ETH Entrepreneur Club in Switzerland, the InCube Challenge is a unique five-day innovation competition where selected participants live and work together inside a transparent glass cube while collaborating to solve pressing challenges presented by major global companies and institutions. Taking place from 26–30 October 2026, this highly competitive program combines entrepreneurship, innovation, teamwork, mentorship, rapid prototyping, networking, leadership development, and live pitching into one unforgettable experience. Unlike traditional hackathons or startup competitions, the InCube Challenge pushes participants beyond their comfort zones by immersing them in a high-intensity environment where creativity, collaboration, and problem-solving happen in real time under public visibility. Applications for the ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026 are open from 1 May to 31 May 2026. What is the ETH InCube Challenge? The InCube Challenge is an immersive innovation competition organized by the ETH Entrepreneur Club, designed to bring together talented individuals from diverse backgrounds to solve practical industry and societal challenges. Participants are grouped into multidisciplinary teams and placed inside specially designed glass cubes for five days. During this period, teams collaborate intensively to: Analyze complex real-world problems Develop innovative solutions Prototype ideas rapidly Receive mentorship from experts and industry leaders Prepare final pitches for judges and audiences The challenge culminates in a live finale where teams present their solutions before executives, innovation leaders, entrepreneurs, judges, and hundreds of students. The program emphasizes: Entrepreneurship Sustainability Innovation Human-centered problem-solving Technology-driven impact Team collaboration Leadership under pressure Why the InCube Challenge Stands Out The ETH InCube Challenge has become one of Europe’s most exciting entrepreneurship and innovation experiences because of its unconventional format and strong industry partnerships. Participants do not simply attend workshops or seminars. Instead, they become fully immersed in a live innovation ecosystem where they must collaborate, think critically, and build practical solutions in real time. The challenge allows participants to: Work directly on real-world industry problems Collaborate with talented peers from around the world Receive mentorship from experienced professionals Gain exposure to multinational companies Build entrepreneurial and leadership skills Expand professional networks Develop creative confidence Experience innovation under pressure Present solutions to high-level audiences Explore Switzerland’s innovation ecosystem The Complete InCube Journey The InCube experience is structured into several stages designed to prepare participants for the final innovation challenge. 1. PrepCube – Virtual Kickoff The journey begins with a virtual onboarding phase where participants: Meet fellow innovators Learn about the challenge themes Understand expectations and workflows Begin early collaboration in immersive online environments 2. OutCube – Swiss Alps Retreat Before entering the glass cube, participants travel to the Swiss Alps for a team-building and preparation retreat. This stage helps participants: Build team chemistry Strengthen collaboration Prepare mentally for the challenge Develop early concepts and approaches Experience Switzerland’s unique environment The OutCube phase is scheduled for 20–24 October 2026. 3. InCube – The Main Challenge The central part of the experience takes place from 26–30 October 2026. Participants: Live inside the cube Collaborate intensively with teammates Attend mentoring sessions Conduct brainstorming and prototyping Develop innovative solutions Work under tight timelines The challenge environment is designed to simulate real startup and innovation pressures. 4. Finale – Pitch and Win At the end of the challenge, teams pitch their solutions before: Industry leaders Corporate executives Innovation experts Entrepreneurs Students Judges The final presentations provide teams with visibility, recognition, networking opportunities, and the possibility of winning prizes. Real Industry Challenges from Global Companies One of the most impressive aspects of the InCube Challenge is its collaboration with major international corporations and institutions. Previous challenge sponsors and partners include: UBS ABB Roche IQVIA Siemens AWS Microsoft Huawei Red Bull Swiss Post Allianz SIX ETH Zurich Axpo Thermo Fisher Scientific Hilti PMI Science BKW Swiss Engineering Participants work on real corporate and societal problems related to: Artificial intelligence Sustainability Healthcare Climate solutions Energy transition Smart buildings Public engagement Green energy Digital innovation Data analytics Patient experience Infrastructure Technology adoption Examples of Past Innovation Challenges Thermo Fisher Scientific Challenge Participants explored how data could help achieve net-zero sustainability goals within clinical trial services. Siemens x AWS Challenge Teams developed AI-driven solutions to improve smart building efficiency, sustainability, and occupant comfort. Roche x IQVIA Challenge Participants worked on improving patient and doctor experiences for lower-priority healthcare products. Axpo Challenge Teams explored ways to accelerate public adoption of sustainable technologies. ABB Challenge Participants focused on solving Switzerland’s energy storage and green energy demand challenges. TransnetBW Challenge Teams examined how young people could become more actively involved in the energy transition. Benefits of Joining the ETH InCube Challenge 2026 Participants in the InCube Challenge gain access to numerous personal and professional benefits. Key Benefits Include: Exposure to global companies and innovation leaders Hands-on experience solving real-world problems Entrepreneurial and innovation training Team collaboration experience Mentorship from experts Networking opportunities Rapid prototyping experience Leadership development International exposure Access to a global innovation community Public speaking and pitching experience Practical experience in design thinking and innovation methodologies Who Should Apply? The ETH InCube Challenge welcomes individuals from diverse academic and professional backgrounds who are passionate about innovation and problem-solving. Ideal applicants include: University students Graduate students Researchers Entrepreneurs Engineers Designers Innovators Startup founders Creatives Scientists Technology enthusiasts Sustainability advocates The program values: Curiosity Creativity Teamwork Leadership potential Problem-solving ability Adaptability Entrepreneurial thinking Important Dates for ETH InCube Challenge 2026 Application Timeline Applications Open: 1 May 2026 Applications Close: 31 May 2026 Group Interviews: June 2026 Individual Interviews: June – July 2026 Final Decisions: August 2026 OutCube Retreat: 20–24 October 2026 InCube Challenge: 26–30 October 2026 Why Switzerland is the Perfect Innovation Hub Switzerland is globally recognized for: Innovation Entrepreneurship Research excellence Sustainability leadership Technology development Hosting the challenge at ETH Zurich and across Swiss innovation locations gives participants exposure to one of the world’s strongest entrepreneurial ecosystems. What Participants Can Expect Participants joining the InCube Challenge can expect: Intense collaboration Real-world impact Fast-paced innovation Exposure to cutting-edge technologies Career development opportunities Global networking Creative experimentation High-energy teamwork Mentorship-driven learning The experience is designed not only to develop solutions, but also to transform participants into future innovators and leaders. How to Apply Interested applicants can apply through the official ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge application portal during the application period from 1 May to 31 May 2026. Applicants should prepare for: Group interviews Individual interviews Collaborative assessments Innovation-focused discussions Strong applications will typically demonstrate: Passion for innovation Team collaboration skills Leadership potential Curiosity and creativity Interest in solving real-world challenges Apply Here Final Thoughts The ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026 is more than an innovation competition. It is an immersive entrepreneurial experience designed to push participants beyond traditional learning environments and into real-world innovation. By combining collaboration, mentorship, sustainability, entrepreneurship, and rapid problem-solving, the challenge creates a powerful environment where participants can develop transformative ideas while building meaningful global connections. For aspiring innovators who want to gain practical experience, work with leading companies, and become part of an international entrepreneurial ecosystem, the InCube Challenge 2026 offers a truly unique opportunity. For more information about this competition visit here To discover more opportunities on OFY, click here The post ETH Entrepreneur Club InCube Challenge 2026: Global Innovation Challenge in Switzerland for Future Entrepreneurs and Problem Solvers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: J-PAL Europe Policy and Research Associate 2026 in France: Fully Detailed Career Opportunity for Early-Career Researchers and Policy Professionals
summary: The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab (J-PAL) Europe has officially opened applications for the prestigious Policy and Research Associate position based in France for 2026. This exciting international opportunity is ideal for early-career professionals who are passionate about evidence-based policymaking, development economics, research, impact evaluation, and public policy. Hosted at the Paris School of Economics, this position offers candidates a unique chance to work at the intersection of research, policy development, training, and international collaboration while contributing to global poverty reduction efforts through rigorous scientific evidence. Applicants interested in policy research jobs in Europe, development economics careers, impact evaluation positions, and international public policy opportunities should pay close attention to this highly competitive role. About J-PAL Europe The Abdul Latif Jameel Poverty Action Lab, widely known as J-PAL, is a globally respected research center dedicated to reducing poverty through evidence-informed policymaking. J-PAL works with a network of more than 1,100 affiliated professors at universities worldwide and is recognized internationally for advancing randomized impact evaluations and rigorous policy research. J-PAL Europe serves as one of J-PAL’s seven regional offices and focuses on generating and utilizing evidence to shape social programs and policies across Europe and beyond. This particular role is connected to the “Development Methodologies Chair,” established jointly by: The French Development Agency (AFD) University Paris Sciences et Lettres (PSL) The chair is led by Nobel Prize-winning economists and J-PAL co-founders: Abhijit Banerjee Esther Duflo The initiative seeks to strengthen research in development economics and improve policymaking through scientific evidence and innovative methodologies. Position Overview Position Title Policy and Research Associate – J-PAL Europe Organization J-PAL Europe Location France (based at the Paris School of Economics) Earliest Start Date 1 August 2026 Latest Start Date 1 September 2026 This role provides hands-on professional experience for individuals interested in: Development economics Public policy Humanitarian policy Impact evaluations Research communication Evidence synthesis Policy outreach International development Research capacity building The selected candidate will support the creation of new research opportunities, strengthen partnerships, coordinate policy outreach, and contribute to training activities. Why This Opportunity Is Important This is not just another research assistant position. The J-PAL Europe Policy and Research Associate role provides direct exposure to: International policymakers Leading economists Evidence-based policy initiatives Research dissemination strategies High-level development institutions Policy conferences and workshops Impact evaluation methodologies The position is particularly valuable for candidates planning to pursue: A PhD in economics or public policy Careers in international organizations Research leadership roles Policy advisory careers Development consulting Humanitarian policy careers Main Responsibilities of the Role The selected candidate will contribute across several important areas. 1. Policy Development and Outreach The associate will support evidence dissemination and policy engagement through: Building relationships with: Governments International organizations NGOs Private-sector institutions Identifying opportunities for rigorous evaluations Preparing policy communication materials such as: Policy briefs Evaluation summaries Presentations Research insights Translating complex academic findings into accessible formats for policymakers Supporting new partnerships that encourage evidence-based policymaking Writing: Blog articles Opinion editorials Public communication materials Coordinating: Conferences Workshops Dissemination events Panels and stakeholder engagements Supporting outreach to: Refugee-led organizations Policy stakeholders Development actors 2. Research Project Development The associate will also help identify and support new impact evaluations by: Identifying promising research opportunities Engaging implementing partners Exploring evaluation strategies Supporting proposal development Coordinating with J-PAL researchers Assisting fundraising efforts where needed This aspect of the role gives candidates direct exposure to real-world research implementation and experimental policy evaluation. 3. Training and Capacity Building The role includes contributions to training programs designed for: Policymakers Project leaders Researchers Users of evidence-based findings Responsibilities include: Supporting training sessions Developing learning materials Helping communicate evaluation methodologies to diverse audiences Professional Development Benefits J-PAL Europe offers extensive opportunities for professional growth. Selected candidates will gain access to: Global Research Networks Candidates will interact with world-leading researchers and development professionals. Advanced Research Training Exposure to: Randomized evaluations Causal inference Quantitative research methodologies Evidence synthesis techniques International Exposure Potential travel opportunities for: Conferences Workshops Policy engagements Dissemination activities Career Advancement The role is especially beneficial for individuals preparing for: PhD programs Research leadership International development careers Public policy careers Eligibility Requirements Candidates must meet several academic and professional requirements. Educational Qualifications Applicants should hold a Master’s degree in: Economics Public policy Or a closely related field Coursework should ideally include: Microeconomics Econometrics Causal inference Candidates from other disciplines may still qualify if they demonstrate: Strong quantitative research interest Knowledge of randomized evaluations Evidence-based policy engagement Required Experience Applicants should have: At least one year of relevant professional experience Relevant experience may include: Research assistantships Research implementation Policy research Impact evaluations Research capacity-building projects Preferred Technical Skills Strong preference will be given to candidates with experience in: Randomized evaluations Impact evaluation methods Quantitative research Evidence synthesis Policy communication Communication Skills Applicants must demonstrate: Excellent English writing skills Strong oral communication abilities Ability to communicate with diverse stakeholders Language Requirements Required Fluency in English Preferred French language skills Other European languages Additional Competencies Ideal applicants should possess: Excellent organizational skills Strong time management Attention to detail Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously Cultural sensitivity Multicultural teamwork experience International collaboration skills Interest or experience in the humanitarian sector is considered an added advantage. Travel Requirements Candidates should be willing and able to travel internationally for project-related activities and events. Application Process Interested applicants must complete the J-PAL/IPA common application. Applicants should: Register on the J-PAL application system Select the “Policy” job category Choose: “Research and Policy Associate J-PAL Europe” Apply Here Required Application Documents Applicants must submit: CV/Resume Cover letter Reference contacts The cover letter should explain: Relevant experience Qualifications Motivation for the role Authorization to work in the European Union Incomplete applications will not be considered. Important Deadline Application Deadline: 31 May 2026 Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted for interviews. Why Candidates Should Apply Early Competitive international research opportunities often receive large application volumes. Applying early may help candidates avoid technical delays and demonstrate strong interest in the position. Career Opportunities After This Role This opportunity can open doors to careers in: International development Academic research Public policy consulting Humanitarian organizations Government advisory work International NGOs Multilateral institutions Economic policy research Many previous J-PAL staff members have gone on to: Top PhD programs International organizations Research institutes Global policy institutions Final Thoughts The J-PAL Europe Policy and Research Associate position in France represents one of the strongest international opportunities available for young professionals interested in research-driven policymaking and development economics. Through direct collaboration with globally recognized researchers and policymakers, candidates will gain exceptional exposure to evidence-based policy work and international development research. For aspiring economists, public policy professionals, researchers, and development practitioners, this role offers both meaningful impact and long-term career growth. For more information on this job visit here Discover more job opportunities on OFY on here The post J-PAL Europe Policy and Research Associate 2026 in France: Fully Detailed Career Opportunity for Early-Career Researchers and Policy Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
